rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative coupling of cyclic beta-keto esters with styrenes to form beta-keto spirolactones via double anodic oxidation and water trapping under green solvent conditions
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the deprotonated beta-keto ester enolate to an alpha-keto radical, followed by a second anodic oxidation of the benzylic radical adduct to a benzylic carbocation; cathode reduces protons/water to H2

key_intermediates.name: alpha-keto radical from beta-keto ester (intermediate I)
benzylic radical adduct after styrene addition (intermediate II)
benzylic alcohol after water trapping (intermediate IV)

electron_flow: Base deprotonates the beta-keto ester; the anode oxidizes the enolate to an alpha-keto radical (I) which adds to the styrene to give a benzylic radical (II); a second anodic oxidation gives a benzylic carbocation (III), which is trapped intermolecularly by water to alcohol IV, and intramolecular lactonization affords the spirolactone, while the cathode reduces protons to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: TEMPO adduct 6 isolated in 89% yield, supporting formation of benzylic radical II; TEMPO adduct 7 of the alpha-keto radical detected by mass spectrometry

probe_reagents.name: H2-18O (18O-labeled water)
probe_reagents.observation: 18O incorporated into the lactone oxygen of product 4 (48% overall yield), demonstrating that water (not the ester) supplies the lactone oxygen, consistent with mechanism A (intermolecular water attack on carbocation)

notes: Two mechanistic pathways are considered after the carbocation III: Mechanism A (favored) involves intermolecular attack of water on the benzylic carbocation to give alcohol IV, followed by intramolecular lactonization; Mechanism B involves intramolecular ester oxygen attack to form oxocarbenium IV' followed by hydrolysis. 18O-labeling experiments support Mechanism A. Isolation of hemiketal byproducts 3s/3t is also consistent with water trapping of the carbocation. Reaction performed in acetone/water with LiClO4 electrolyte, 2,6-lutidine base, graphite anode and Ni-foam cathode, runs under batch and continuous flow.
